offerings.label: Product leadership, at every depth
offerings.heading: Ways to put senior product leadership on your team.

items.title: Fractional CPO
items.subtitle: Full ownership of the product function — strategy through execution.
items.body: Scaled to your inflection point: own strategy, align go-to-market, and drive execution — embedded in your team, operating on your cadence, without the cost or ramp of a full-time hire.
triggers: Founder-led product — ready to hand it to a seasoned operator
Scale and complexity have outgrown current product leadership
CPO-caliber experience — without the full-time hire

items.title: Strategic Advisor
items.subtitle: Your team owns the build — I pressure-test direction and sharpen decisions.
items.body: Product strategy definition and review, critical decision support, and revising Ways of Working — high-leverage access when it matters most.
triggers: Strategy is in motion — and direction still feels uncertain
A high-stakes bet on the table: raise, new market, or strategic pivot
You're shipping — but it's not showing up in retention or revenue

items.title: Executive Operator
items.subtitle: Strategic judgment at execution speed — one initiative, driven to launch.
items.body: What slows launches is rarely the building — it's the unmade decisions: scope, sequencing, positioning, what good looks like. I take ownership of a single bet — an AI-native product, a new product line — making the strategic calls in real time and driving design, build, and iteration to launch.
triggers: A strategic initiative on the table — and no senior owner to drive it
Deep technical capability that needs translating into a product people actually use
Delivery keeps stalling on decisions only a product executive can make

items.title: Craft Coach
items.subtitle: Level up your product leaders and teams already doing the work.
items.body: Targeted 1:1 coaching and team workshops, built around where your leaders and teams need extra support.
triggers: Develop your product leader for the next step

items.label: ARR growth at Monday.com, scaled past $1B ARR
Churn reduction at BQE Software in under 10 months

about.label: Who I am
about.heading: Meet Guy Assedou.
about.subtitle: The operator behind the practice.
about.quote: What drives me is helping leaders realize their vision and build products that create real impact.

points.title: The inflection point is the job
points.body: The most valuable company moments happen at transition — when the foundation exists but the path forward isn't clear. The work is knowing where to focus and how to make every bet move the metrics that matter.

points.title: Pattern recognition, earned
points.body: Built across B2B SaaS, B2C2C, and consumer — global enterprises and early-stage startups, horizontal and vertical markets. Breadth across distinct contexts is what turns experience into instinct.

points.title: Built for the US market
points.body: A decade embedded inside American SaaS — not advising from a distance, but building. GTM dynamics, investor framing, cultural context: I know this market from the inside.

points.title: Close to the craft
points.body: I mentor PMs and speak at ProductCon LA, ProductWorld, and Product-Led Summit. Staying close to how good product thinking spreads keeps what I bring you sharp.

about.credentials: Kellogg MBA · Technion BS EE · IDF Major

items.q: What types of companies do you work with?
items.a: Growth-stage B2B SaaS companies — typically Series A to Series C — that are navigating a product inflection point. That might be scaling past product-market fit, entering a new market, rebuilding a team, or preparing for a raise.

items.q: How is this different from hiring a full-time CPO?
items.a: You get senior product leadership — strategy, execution, and team development — without the 12-month ramp, equity dilution, or full-time cost. Engagements are scoped to what you actually need, and can scale up or down as the business evolves.

items.q: What does a typical engagement look like?
items.a: Most engagements start with a focused strategy sprint — understanding the business, the product, and the gaps — then move into an ongoing operating rhythm: weekly leadership, prioritization, and cross-functional alignment. The exact shape depends on where you are and what you need.

items.q: Do you work with multiple companies simultaneously?
items.a: Yes, and deliberately so. Exposure to multiple growth-stage companies sharpens pattern recognition — what works, what doesn't, and how to move faster. I keep engagements limited to ensure each gets real attention.

items.q: How quickly can we get started?
items.a: Typically within 1–2 weeks. The first step is a 30-minute call to understand the situation and see if it's a good fit. If it is, we move fast.
Minimum three months. Real product leadership takes time to create durable results — a one-off advisory session is a different product. That said, strategy-only retainers can start shorter.

cta.label: Ready to find your inflection point?
cta.body: 15+ years of product leadership, on your team — without the full-time overhead.
